summary: Iteration 28 — RAG-memory plumbing for /api/research and Research Memory settings panel. Backend: 6/6 pytest cases in test_research_memory.py pass; live POST /api/research with memory=[] and memory=[{...}] returns 401 (auth) — schema accepts the new optional memory field (no 422). Frontend: research-memory-panel renders in Settings with correct empty/singular/plural count text ('0 notes stored', '1 note stored', '3 notes stored'), Clear button only appears when count>0, confirm-dialog clear flow wipes localStorage key 'mindmapper.researchMemory.v1' and resets count to 0. Landing CTA regression verified: [data-testid='nav-launch-btn'] renders 'Try Free' (not 'Launch App').
critical_code_review_comments: ResearchSettings refreshes memoryCount only on mount/focus/storage events — works because Settings modal unmounts on close, so re-opening re-reads localStorage. If you ever convert it to always-mounted, add a visibility/open-prop dependency to re-tick.
countResearchMemories() is called synchronously in useState initializer — fine for small JSON, but if MAX_ENTRIES grows beyond 50 consider lazy/idle loading.
window.confirm is used for Clear — acceptable for now; a custom modal would give better UX + easier testing without page.on('dialog') dance.
test_credentials: App gate: ?unlock=mind-mapper67 (no user login needed to reach Studio/Settings).
seed_data_creation: Injected ephemeral entries into localStorage key 'mindmapper.researchMemory.v1' during frontend test; cleared at end of flow. No persistent seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: RAG-memory plumbing end-to-end verified. Backend model (ResearchRequest.memory: List[ResearchMemoryEntry]) is accepted on the live API — authenticated runs were NOT exercised (would trigger real LLM). If future tests want to verify prompt injection, stub out call_llm and assert build_memory_blob output appears in user_text for both /api/research and /api/research/stream. Frontend library /app/frontend/src/lib/researchMemory.js exports (appendResearchMemory, findRelatedMemories, buildMemoryContext, countResearchMemories, clearResearchMemory) were exercised indirectly via ResearchSettings; a direct unit-ish check could be added by importing them in a Studio console eval.
